tracks.label: Foundations
tracks.description: Core concepts every AI practitioner must know

tracks.label: Practitioner
tracks.description: Applied skills for building with AI

tracks.label: Production
tracks.description: Deploying and operating AI systems reliably

topics.title: What is a Neural Network?
topics.summary: Layers of connected nodes that learn patterns from data by adjusting weights during training.

topics.title: Supervised vs Unsupervised Learning
topics.summary: Supervised learning uses labeled examples; unsupervised learning finds structure in unlabeled data.

topics.title: What is a Large Language Model?
topics.summary: A neural network trained on massive text corpora to predict and generate human language.

topics.title: Tokens and Tokenization
topics.summary: LLMs process text as tokens — subword chunks — not raw characters or whole words.

topics.title: The Transformer Architecture
topics.summary: The attention-based architecture introduced in 2017 that underlies virtually every modern LLM.

topics.title: Attention Mechanism
topics.summary: Allows the model to weigh how relevant each token is to every other token when generating output.

topics.title: Context Window
topics.summary: The maximum number of tokens a model can process at once — both input and output combined.

topics.title: Temperature and Sampling
topics.summary: Temperature controls randomness in token selection; higher values produce more creative, less predictable output.

topics.title: Top-p and Top-k Sampling
topics.summary: Sampling strategies that limit token selection to the most probable candidates, controlling output diversity.

topics.title: Pre-training vs Fine-tuning
topics.summary: Pre-training builds general knowledge from massive data; fine-tuning adapts the model to specific tasks.

topics.title: RLHF — Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
topics.summary: Training technique that uses human preference ratings to align model outputs with desired behavior.

topics.title: Embeddings
topics.summary: Dense numerical vectors that represent the semantic meaning of text, enabling similarity comparisons.

topics.title: Vector Similarity and Cosine Distance
topics.summary: Measuring how semantically similar two embeddings are using the angle between their vectors.

topics.title: Loss Functions
topics.summary: Mathematical functions that quantify how far a model's predictions are from the correct answers during training.

topics.title: Gradient Descent
topics.summary: The optimization algorithm that iteratively adjusts model weights to minimize the loss function.

topics.title: Backpropagation
topics.summary: Algorithm for computing gradients through a network by propagating errors from output back to input layers.

topics.title: Overfitting and Regularization
topics.summary: Overfitting is memorizing training data; regularization techniques like dropout prevent this.

topics.title: Batch Size and Learning Rate
topics.summary: Key hyperparameters: batch size controls training stability; learning rate controls how fast weights update.

topics.title: Model Parameters vs Hyperparameters
topics.summary: Parameters are learned during training; hyperparameters are set before training and control the process.

topics.title: Benchmarks — MMLU, HumanEval, HellaSwag
topics.summary: Standardized evaluation datasets used to compare model capabilities across reasoning, coding, and comprehension.

topics.title: Hallucination
topics.summary: When a model generates plausible-sounding but factually incorrect or fabricated information.

topics.title: Grounding
topics.summary: Connecting model outputs to verified external sources to reduce hallucination and improve factual accuracy.

topics.title: Zero-shot vs Few-shot Learning
topics.summary: Zero-shot uses no examples; few-shot provides a small number of examples in the prompt to guide the model.

topics.title: In-context Learning
topics.summary: The model's ability to adapt its behavior based on examples or instructions provided in the prompt itself.

topics.title: Multimodal Models
topics.summary: Models that process and generate multiple data types — text, images, audio, or video — in a single system.

topics.title: Model Size and Scaling Laws
topics.summary: Larger models trained on more data consistently perform better — a relationship described by empirical scaling laws.

topics.title: Mixture of Experts (MoE)
topics.summary: Architecture where only a subset of model parameters are activated per token, enabling larger models efficiently.

topics.title: Positional Encoding
topics.summary: Mechanism that injects token order information into transformer inputs, since attention is inherently order-agnostic.

topics.title: Layer Normalization
topics.summary: Normalization technique applied within transformer layers to stabilize and accelerate training.

topics.title: Feed-forward Layers in Transformers
topics.summary: The MLP component in each transformer block that processes each token independently after attention.

topics.title: Encoder vs Decoder Models
topics.summary: Encoders (BERT) understand text; decoders (GPT) generate text; encoder-decoders (T5) do both.

topics.title: Autoregressive Generation
topics.summary: Generating text one token at a time, with each new token conditioned on all previously generated tokens.

topics.title: Softmax Function
topics.summary: Converts raw model scores (logits) into a probability distribution over the vocabulary for next-token selection.

topics.title: Cross-entropy Loss
topics.summary: The standard loss function for language model training, measuring divergence between predicted and actual token distributions.

topics.title: Knowledge Cutoff
topics.summary: The date after which a model has no training data — events after this date are unknown to the model.

topics.title: Model Weights and Checkpoints
topics.summary: Weights are the learned parameters; checkpoints are saved snapshots of weights at points during training.

topics.title: Inference vs Training
topics.summary: Training updates model weights using data; inference uses fixed weights to generate predictions.

topics.title: Quantization
topics.summary: Reducing model precision (e.g. 32-bit to 4-bit) to shrink memory footprint and speed up inference.

topics.title: Distillation
topics.summary: Training a smaller student model to mimic the behavior of a larger teacher model.

topics.title: Sparse vs Dense Models
topics.summary: Dense models activate all parameters per token; sparse models (MoE) activate only a fraction.

topics.title: Logits
topics.summary: Raw, unnormalized scores output by the final layer of a model before softmax converts them to probabilities.

topics.title: Perplexity
topics.summary: A metric for language model quality — lower perplexity means the model is less surprised by real text.

topics.title: Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
topics.summary: Prompting technique where the model reasons step-by-step before giving a final answer, improving accuracy.

topics.title: Constitutional AI
topics.summary: Anthropic's technique for training AI to self-critique and revise outputs according to a set of principles.

topics.title: Instruction Tuning
topics.summary: Fine-tuning a base model on instruction-response pairs so it follows natural language directives.

topics.title: System Prompt
topics.summary: Instructions given to a model before the conversation begins, shaping its persona, constraints, and behavior.

topics.title: Prompt Injection
topics.summary: An attack where malicious text in input overrides or manipulates the system prompt or intended behavior.

topics.title: Emergent Capabilities
topics.summary: Abilities that appear in large models that were not present in smaller versions and were not explicitly trained.

topics.title: AI Alignment
topics.summary: The challenge of ensuring AI systems pursue goals that are beneficial and consistent with human values.

topics.title: The Turing Test
topics.summary: A benchmark proposed by Alan Turing in 1950 where a machine passes if it is indistinguishable from a human in text conversation.

topics.title: Prompt Engineering Fundamentals
topics.summary: The craft of structuring inputs to reliably elicit desired outputs from language models.

topics.title: System Prompts in Practice
topics.summary: How to write effective system prompts that define model persona, scope, tone, and constraints.

topics.title: Few-shot Prompting Patterns
topics.summary: Providing worked examples in the prompt to demonstrate the exact format and reasoning style you want.

topics.title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting
topics.summary: Asking the model to show its reasoning steps, improving accuracy on math, logic, and multi-step tasks.

topics.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
topics.summary: Combining a retrieval system with an LLM so answers are grounded in fetched, up-to-date documents.

topics.title: Chunking Strategies for RAG
topics.summary: How to split documents into chunks — by size, sentence, or semantic unit — for effective retrieval.

topics.title: Embedding Models for Retrieval
topics.summary: Choosing and using embedding models (OpenAI, Cohere, BGE) to index and query your document store.

topics.title: Vector Databases in Practice
topics.summary: Using Chroma, Pinecone, or Qdrant to store embeddings and run fast similarity search at scale.

topics.title: Tool Use and Function Calling
topics.summary: Giving models the ability to invoke external functions — APIs, calculators, search — to extend their capabilities.

topics.title: Structured Output with JSON Mode
topics.summary: Constraining model output to valid JSON for reliable downstream parsing in applications.

topics.title: LLM APIs — OpenAI, Anthropic, Google
topics.summary: Core differences in API design, pricing models, rate limits, and capability trade-offs across major providers.

topics.title: Building a Chatbot with Memory
topics.summary: Implementing conversation history management to maintain context across multi-turn interactions.

topics.title: Streaming Responses
topics.summary: Using server-sent events to stream tokens as they are generated for a faster, more responsive UX.

topics.title: Token Counting and Cost Management
topics.summary: Estimating token usage before API calls and controlling costs through truncation and caching strategies.

topics.title: Prompt Templates and Variables
topics.summary: Parameterizing prompts with dynamic values using template strings for reusable, maintainable prompt code.

topics.title: Output Parsing and Validation
topics.summary: Reliably extracting structured data from model responses and handling malformed outputs gracefully.

topics.title: LangChain Core Concepts
topics.summary: Chains, agents, tools, and memory — the four primitives of the LangChain framework.

topics.title: LlamaIndex for Document Q&A
topics.summary: Using LlamaIndex to ingest, index, and query large document collections with LLMs.

topics.title: Fine-tuning with LoRA
topics.summary: Low-Rank Adaptation enables efficient fine-tuning by training only a small number of additional parameters.

topics.title: Dataset Preparation for Fine-tuning
topics.summary: Formatting instruction-response pairs into JSONL and cleaning data for supervised fine-tuning.

topics.title: Evaluation — Building an LLM Test Suite
topics.summary: Writing deterministic and LLM-as-judge evaluations to measure model quality before shipping.

topics.title: Prompt Versioning and Management
topics.summary: Treating prompts as code — version control, A/B testing, and regression testing for prompt changes.

topics.title: Agentic Loops
topics.summary: Architectures where an LLM repeatedly reasons, acts, and observes until a task is complete.

topics.title: ReAct Pattern
topics.summary: Reason + Act: the model alternates between reasoning steps and tool-use actions to solve tasks.

topics.title: Multi-agent Architectures
topics.summary: Systems with multiple specialized LLM agents collaborating, each with distinct roles and tools.

topics.title: Running Local Models with Ollama
topics.summary: Using Ollama to run Llama, Mistral, and other open models on your laptop without cloud API costs.

topics.title: Comparing Models for a Task
topics.summary: Systematic approach to selecting the right model — capability, latency, cost, and context window trade-offs.

topics.title: Image Input with Vision Models
topics.summary: Sending images to multimodal APIs (GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini) for analysis, captioning, and extraction.

topics.title: Building a PDF Q&A System
topics.summary: Extract, chunk, embed, and query PDF content using RAG to answer questions over documents.

topics.title: Web Scraping for LLM Context
topics.summary: Fetching and cleaning web content to use as real-time context in LLM prompts.

topics.title: Semantic Search vs Keyword Search
topics.summary: Semantic search uses embeddings to find meaning; keyword search matches exact terms — both have trade-offs.

topics.title: Hybrid Search
topics.summary: Combining dense vector search with sparse BM25 keyword search for better retrieval coverage.

topics.title: Re-ranking Retrieved Results
topics.summary: Using a cross-encoder or LLM to re-score retrieved chunks by relevance before passing them to the model.

topics.title: Summarization Strategies
topics.summary: Map-reduce, refine, and rolling summarization patterns for handling long documents that exceed context limits.

topics.title: Code Generation Best Practices
topics.summary: Prompting techniques for reliable code output — including test-first generation and iterative debugging loops.

topics.title: LLM Output Caching
topics.summary: Caching identical or semantically similar prompts to reduce latency and API costs in production.

topics.title: Guardrails and Input Validation
topics.summary: Filtering and validating user inputs before they reach the model to prevent misuse and prompt injection.

topics.title: Building a Classifier with an LLM
topics.summary: Using an LLM with a structured prompt to categorize, route, or label text without training a custom model.

topics.title: Named Entity Recognition with LLMs
topics.summary: Extracting structured entities (names, dates, amounts) from unstructured text using prompted LLMs.

topics.title: Sentiment Analysis with LLMs
topics.summary: Classifying emotional tone in text — and getting confidence scores and explanations — using LLM prompts.

topics.title: Translation with LLMs
topics.summary: Using LLMs for nuanced translation that preserves tone, idiom, and domain-specific terminology.

topics.title: Data Extraction from Unstructured Text
topics.summary: Pulling structured fields from emails, reports, and documents using prompted LLMs with JSON output.

topics.title: LLM-based Routing
topics.summary: Using a fast, cheap LLM to classify user intent and route requests to specialized downstream handlers.

topics.title: Fallback and Retry Strategies
topics.summary: Handling API failures, timeouts, and malformed outputs gracefully with exponential backoff and fallback models.

topics.title: Batch Processing with LLMs
topics.summary: Processing large volumes of documents asynchronously using batch API endpoints to reduce cost and latency.

topics.title: System Design for LLM Applications
topics.summary: Architectural patterns for building scalable, maintainable LLM apps — separation of concerns and state management.

topics.title: Evaluating RAG Systems
topics.summary: Metrics for RAG quality: retrieval precision, answer faithfulness, and answer relevance.

topics.title: Context Stuffing vs RAG Trade-offs
topics.summary: When to stuff all context into a long-context model vs when to use retrieval — cost, latency, and accuracy.

topics.title: Building with the Vercel AI SDK
topics.summary: Using the Vercel AI SDK for streaming, tool use, and multi-provider model switching in TypeScript web apps.

topics.title: Cursor and AI-native Development
topics.summary: How AI-native IDEs like Cursor change the development workflow with codebase-aware chat and autocomplete.

topics.title: Latency Optimization for LLM APIs
topics.summary: Techniques to reduce response time: streaming, caching, smaller models, and prompt length reduction.

topics.title: LLM Observability and Tracing
topics.summary: Using tools like LangSmith, Helicone, or Langfuse to trace every LLM call, input, output, and cost.

topics.title: Rate Limiting and Quota Management
topics.summary: Handling API rate limits gracefully with queues, backoff, and per-user quotas in your application.

topics.title: Prompt Security in Production
topics.summary: Preventing prompt injection, jailbreaks, and data leakage in user-facing LLM applications.

topics.title: Cost Monitoring and Budgets
topics.summary: Setting spend alerts, tracking per-request costs, and choosing models based on cost-performance trade-offs.

topics.title: A/B Testing Prompts
topics.summary: Running controlled experiments to compare prompt variants on real user traffic before full rollout.

topics.title: Model Versioning and Rollback
topics.summary: Pinning to specific model versions and maintaining rollback capability when providers release updates.

topics.title: Human-in-the-Loop Workflows
topics.summary: Designing systems where humans review, correct, or approve AI outputs before they take effect.

topics.title: Confidence Scoring and Uncertainty
topics.summary: Estimating how confident an LLM is in its output and routing low-confidence cases to human review.

topics.title: LLM Output Moderation
topics.summary: Using moderation APIs or a secondary LLM to filter harmful, off-topic, or policy-violating outputs.

topics.title: Data Privacy in LLM Applications
topics.summary: PII detection, data minimization, and ensuring user data is not inadvertently sent to third-party model APIs.

topics.title: Serving Open-source Models at Scale
topics.summary: Deploying Llama or Mistral with vLLM, TGI, or Modal for high-throughput, low-latency inference.

topics.title: vLLM and PagedAttention
topics.summary: vLLM's memory management technique that enables significantly higher throughput for batched inference.

topics.title: Kubernetes for AI Workloads
topics.summary: Orchestrating GPU nodes, model servers, and autoscaling for AI inference in Kubernetes.

topics.title: Continuous Evaluation in CI/CD
topics.summary: Running LLM eval suites automatically on every deployment to catch regressions before they reach users.

topics.title: Feedback Loops and Data Flywheels
topics.summary: Collecting user feedback to continuously improve model quality through fine-tuning and prompt iteration.

topics.title: Multi-tenant LLM Architecture
topics.summary: Isolating prompts, context, and data between customers in a shared LLM backend.

topics.title: Edge Inference
topics.summary: Running small quantized models on-device (mobile, browser, IoT) to eliminate latency and preserve privacy.

topics.title: Fallback Model Chains
topics.summary: Routing to a cheaper model first and escalating to a more capable one only when needed.

topics.title: SLAs for AI Features
topics.summary: Setting and monitoring latency, uptime, and quality SLAs for AI-powered features in production products.

topics.title: Semantic Caching
topics.summary: Caching LLM responses based on embedding similarity of prompts — not just exact string matches.

topics.title: Load Testing LLM Applications
topics.summary: Simulating concurrent users to measure throughput, latency percentiles, and failure modes under load.

topics.title: Prompt Injection Defenses
topics.summary: Architectural and prompt-level defenses against adversarial inputs designed to hijack model behavior.

topics.title: Compliance and Audit Logging
topics.summary: Logging every LLM interaction with user ID, prompt, response, and timestamp for regulatory compliance.

topics.title: GPU Memory Management
topics.summary: Understanding VRAM requirements for inference and techniques like KV cache management to maximize throughput.

topics.title: Speculative Decoding
topics.summary: Using a small draft model to propose tokens that a larger model verifies in parallel, speeding up inference.

topics.title: Model Routing by Task
topics.summary: Dynamically selecting the right model (fast/cheap vs capable/expensive) based on detected task complexity.

topics.title: Feature Flags for AI
topics.summary: Using feature flags to gradually roll out new models or prompts to subsets of users.

topics.title: Incident Response for AI Systems
topics.summary: Runbooks and protocols for handling AI-specific incidents: hallucination spikes, toxicity, and API outages.

topics.title: LLM Infrastructure Cost Architecture
topics.summary: Designing infrastructure to balance inference cost, latency, and reliability across cloud and self-hosted options.

topics.title: Token Streaming Architecture
topics.summary: Backend and frontend patterns for reliably delivering streamed tokens to users with proper error handling.

topics.title: Canary Deployments for Models
topics.summary: Rolling out new model versions to a small percentage of traffic first to catch quality regressions early.

topics.title: Shadow Mode Testing
topics.summary: Running a new model alongside the current one on live traffic to compare outputs before switching.

topics.title: Retrieval Latency Optimization
topics.summary: Techniques to reduce vector search latency: index tuning, ANN algorithms, and approximate search.

topics.title: Multi-region LLM Deployment
topics.summary: Deploying model endpoints across regions to reduce latency and ensure data residency compliance.

topics.title: Input and Output Schema Validation
topics.summary: Enforcing strict schemas on LLM inputs and outputs using Pydantic or Zod to catch errors early.

topics.title: Async LLM Request Handling
topics.summary: Using async/await patterns and job queues to handle LLM requests without blocking application threads.

topics.title: Cold Start Optimization
topics.summary: Reducing model initialization time on serverless inference platforms through pre-warming and caching.

topics.title: Monitoring Embedding Drift
topics.summary: Detecting when the distribution of user queries shifts away from the data used to build your retrieval index.

topics.title: LLMOps Platforms Overview
topics.summary: Comparing LLMOps tools — LangSmith, Weights & Biases, Helicone, Arize — for production monitoring.

topics.summary: Managing rapidly evolving AI library versions and avoiding breaking changes in LangChain, OpenAI SDK, etc.

topics.title: Graceful Degradation in AI Features
topics.summary: Designing AI features to fall back to rule-based behavior when the model is unavailable or produces low-quality output.

topics.title: API Gateway Patterns for LLMs
topics.summary: Using an API gateway layer to centralize auth, rate limiting, logging, and model routing for LLM backends.

topics.summary: Strategies for when input exceeds the model's context limit: truncation, summarization, and chunking.

topics.title: Evaluating LLM Quality at Scale
topics.summary: Running automated evals across thousands of test cases using LLM-as-judge and human spot-checks.

topics.title: GDPR and AI Data Handling
topics.summary: Requirements for AI systems processing EU user data: consent, purpose limitation, and data subject rights.

topics.title: Prompt Length vs Quality Trade-offs
topics.summary: Longer prompts improve instruction clarity but increase latency and cost — finding the optimal balance.

topics.title: Model Card Reading
topics.summary: Understanding what model cards tell you about intended use, limitations, and evaluation results.

topics.title: AI Feature Rollout Checklist
topics.summary: Pre-launch checklist covering eval, moderation, privacy, cost, and monitoring for AI features.

topics.title: Post-mortems for AI Incidents
topics.summary: Writing blameless post-mortems for AI system failures with a focus on systemic improvements.

topics.title: GPT-4o and Omni Models
topics.summary: Natively multimodal models that process and generate text, audio, and images in a unified architecture.

topics.title: Claude's Extended Thinking
topics.summary: Anthropic's approach to giving Claude dedicated reasoning time before responding to improve accuracy.

topics.title: OpenAI o1 and Reasoning Models
topics.summary: Models trained to reason extensively before answering, with major gains on math and logical tasks.

topics.summary: Google's model with 1M+ token context, enabling analysis of entire codebases, films, or book collections.

topics.summary: How GPT-4, Mixtral, and other frontier models use MoE to achieve high capability with manageable compute.

topics.title: Multimodal Understanding vs Generation
topics.summary: The distinction between models that understand multiple modalities and those that generate across them.

topics.title: AI Agents in the Wild
topics.summary: Real deployments of autonomous AI agents — from coding assistants to customer service — and their failure modes.

topics.title: Computer Use and GUI Agents
topics.summary: Models that can see a screen and control a computer, enabling automation of any desktop task.

topics.summary: OpenAI Sora and competing models that generate temporally consistent, high-fidelity video from text.

topics.summary: AI systems like AlphaProof that can formally verify and discover mathematical proofs.

topics.summary: Using RL reward signals — process and outcome — to train models with improved step-by-step reasoning.

topics.summary: Architectures where AI systems generate training data or improve their own weights — and the risks involved.

topics.summary: AI systems that build internal simulations of physical or social environments to enable planning and prediction.

topics.summary: Using sparse autoencoders to decompose LLM activations into human-interpretable features.

topics.summary: Reverse-engineering the internal computations of neural networks to understand what they are actually doing.

topics.summary: The phenomenon where models represent more features than they have neurons by overlapping representations.

topics.summary: Training models using AI feedback rather than human feedback, scaling alignment with AI-generated critiques.

topics.summary: How labs like Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google evaluate models for dangerous capabilities before release.

topics.summary: AI systems that master complex negotiation and strategy games requiring long-horizon planning and deception.

topics.summary: The ongoing debate about whether continued scaling of data and compute will lead to AGI or hit fundamental limits.

topics.summary: Spending more compute at inference time — through search, sampling, and reasoning — rather than just training.

topics.summary: Emerging protocols and architectures for AI agents to coordinate and communicate with each other directly.

topics.summary: Mamba and S4 models as efficient alternatives to transformers for long-sequence modeling.

topics.summary: International efforts to regulate frontier AI development, from the EU AI Act to the Seoul AI Safety Summit.

topics.summary: How distillation and reasoning training are bringing o1-class reasoning capabilities to smaller, cheaper models.

topics.summary: Models that can process and reason over long-form video for summarization, search, and question answering.

topics.summary: Architectures for AI systems that maintain persistent memory across thousands of interactions with a user.

topics.summary: Directly modifying model activations at inference time to steer behavior without changing weights.

topics.summary: Using tree-of-thought and Monte Carlo tree search with LLMs to solve multi-step planning problems.

topics.summary: Connecting visual and linguistic representations so models develop shared meaning across modalities.

topics.summary: The ARC-AGI benchmark and why it has remained one of the hardest tests for general AI reasoning.

topics.summary: How AI is accelerating the design of the next generation of chips, creating a feedback loop for AI progress.

topics.title: In-context Reinforcement Learning
topics.summary: Models that can perform RL-style adaptation purely from examples in their context window.

topics.summary: How researchers define AGI, what milestones matter, and the disagreements about how close we are.

topics.summary: Combining weights from multiple fine-tuned models to produce a single model with multiple capabilities.

topics.title: Continual Learning
topics.summary: Training models to learn new information without forgetting previously learned knowledge — avoiding catastrophic forgetting.

topics.summary: Paul Christiano's proposal to use AI-generated debate as a scalable method for human oversight of capable AI.

topics.summary: How AI coding tools are changing developer productivity, code review, and the skills that matter.

topics.summary: Deploying LLMs for tier-1 support deflection, agent assist, and automated ticket resolution.

topics.summary: LLMs and diagnostic AI helping clinicians with differential diagnosis, summarization, and protocol adherence.

topics.summary: Using LLMs to review, redline, and extract obligations from contracts at scale.

topics.summary: ML models for real-time transaction anomaly detection and adaptive fraud pattern recognition.

topics.summary: Automating candidate screening with LLMs and the bias, compliance, and fairness issues this raises.

topics.title: AI in Marketing — Content Generation
